title: Review this uncommitted diff for bugs, security issues, and code quality. Be concise (max 10 lines).
summary_text: - High: [x_poster.py:55](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/x_poster.py#L55) changes `extract_quote_tweet_id()` to keep the status URL in the tweet body. That contradicts the documented scheduler behavior and risks double-rendering/ugly body text when also sending `quote_tweet_id`; the previous cleanup logic looked intentional. - Medium: [scheduler.py:530](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L530) now drops the entire reply chain if `reply_text` contains any quote URL. If `reply_url` is multiline and includes both a quote URL and a real source URL, all citations are skipped instead of filtering only the quote URL line. - Medium: [tests/test_quote_tweet.py:117](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_quote_tweet.py#L117) rewrites tests to assert the new “keep URL in text” behavior, so the suite now codifies the regression rather than protecting against it. - No obvious new security issue in this diff; main concern is behavior regression around posting/citation handling.
